source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Fall Through Open Hole on Drillship

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Walker Ridge Block 51

actions_taken: IP was evaluated by rig medic and evacuated to onshore medical facility
Area was secured to prevent further access
Permanent grating installed over open hole
Access ladder removed to prevent reoccurrence
BSEE Houma District notified orally and written report submitted within 15 days
event.summary: On March 28, 2024, the Transocean Subsea Superintendent fell approximately nine feet through an open hole on a BOP work platform on the drillship Transocean Deepwater Atlas. The hatch cover had been removed after its hinge separated, creating a dropped object hazard, but no barrier was installed and the open hole was not communicated to the work team. The IP was wearing a safety harness but was not tied off. He injured his knee and shin and was evacuated for medical treatment. The remote control box he was carrying fell an additional 40 feet. Investigation found failures in communication, hazard identification, and implementation of corrective actions.
recommendations: BSEE Houma District has no recommendations for the Office of Incident Investigations at this time

hazards.name: Working at Height on BOP Work Platform

threats.name: Hatch cover hinge separation creating open hole
Removal of damaged hatch cover without installing barrier
Failure to communicate open hole to work team
Worker not tied off while working at height

consequences.name: Personnel injury from fall (knee injury and shin abrasions)
Dropped object (remote control box fell 40 feet)

controls.name: Hatch cover with hinge securing mechanism
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent access to open hole
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier designed to prevent fall hazard

supporting_text: A loose piece of grating covering the hole for the access ladder on the work platform was found damaged
The hinge securing the hatch cover to the work platform separated
Poor design of the hinge connecting the hatch cover to the work platform. The hinge separated from the hatch cover

controls.name: Pre-lift inspection and hazard identification
controls.barrier_role: Detection and identification of hazards before work begins
controls.lod_basis: Verification activity to detect equipment defects before use

supporting_text: During the pre-lift check, a loose piece of grating covering the hole for the access ladder on the work platform was found damaged
The damaged hatch cover was removed, work platform was lifted, and installed on the subsea BOP. Access to the open hole was not prevented
Human Performance Error - Failure to implement corrective actions for missing hatch cover

controls.name: Installation of temporary barrier or barricade over open hole
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent access after hatch removal
controls.lod_basis: Compensatory measure when primary barrier is removed

supporting_text: The damaged hatch cover was removed, work platform was lifted, and installed on the subsea BOP. Access to the open hole was not prevented
Human Performance Error - Failure to put barriers in place prior to removal of hatch cover
Removing damaged hatch cover without a barricade in place

controls.name: Communication of hazards to all work team members
controls.barrier_role: Information transfer to ensure situational awareness
controls.lod_basis: Communication protocol to inform workers of changing conditions

supporting_text: The open hole was not communicated to all personnel involved in the job
The roustabout on the lifting team did not communicate the open hole to all members of the Subsea BOP team after removing the damaged hatch cover
Communication – Instructions not understood
Failure to communicate the open hole led the IP to believe he could enter the work platform without tying off his harness

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for BOP maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Systematic hazard identification and mitigation planning
controls.lod_basis: Planning activity to identify and control hazards before work

supporting_text: The inspectors requested the Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for the subsea BOP maintenance job task, but it could not be provided
Although a Hazard Identification Prompt Card was documented, it did not identify the open hole
If a job task hazard analysis was completed the open hole would have been recognized as a hazard and mitigations would have been put in place
Management Systems – No Hazards analysis - The IP failed to conduct a proper hazard assessment to identify the open hole prior to conducting BOP maintenance

controls.name: Procedures for working at heights with hazard identification and mitigations
controls.barrier_role: Documented guidance for safe work execution
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to guide worker behavior

supporting_text: Procedures were available but did not have hazard identification, hazard mitigations or a place for signatures for personnel performing the work
The IP failed to address the no drop tool policy addressed in the procedures for 'Working at Heights on BOP / Related Equipment or in Subsea areas'
Not following company procedures

controls.name: Fall protection - Safety harness tied off
controls.barrier_role: Personal protective equipment to arrest fall
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to prevent injury if fall occurs

supporting_text: Although the IP was wearing a safety harness, he was not tied off
Work Environment – PPE - Failure to tie off fall protection
No fall protection
Failure to communicate the open hole led the IP to believe he could enter the work platform without tying off his harness

controls.name: Guardrails on work platform
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent falls from platform edges
controls.lod_basis: Passive engineering control to prevent falls

supporting_text: A BOP work platform, consisting of an upper and lower level, with guardrails, needed to be installed on the subsea BOP

controls.name: Onboard medic for emergency response
controls.barrier_role: Emergency medical response to treat injuries

supporting_text: IP was examined by the onboard medic and then evacuated to an onshore medical facility for further treatment
The IP was evaluated by the rig medic, and he was sent in on a helicopter to an onshore medical facility for further evaluation

controls.name: Helicopter evacuation capability
controls.barrier_role: Emergency transport to advanced medical care
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident response to minimize harm through rapid transport

supporting_text: IP was examined by the onboard medic and then evacuated to an onshore medical facility for further treatment
The IP was evaluated by the rig medic, and he was sent in on a helicopter to an onshore medical facility for further evaluation

controls.name: Dropped object prevention - securing tools and equipment
controls.barrier_role: Prevent objects from falling and causing injury or damage
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent dropped objects

supporting_text: The IP failed to address the no drop tool policy addressed in the procedures for 'Working at Heights on BOP / Related Equipment or in Subsea areas'
During the fall, the remote-control box was dropped outside of the work platform and fell approximately 40 feet to the next deck below
The hatch cover, 2' 8.75" x 2' 5.5" x 1", was damaged and a possible dropped object

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
